titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 576

p.seg: Malcolm, king of the Scots, to the sheriff of Berwick and all worthy men and officials, greeting.

Know me to have received the church of Coldingham and the men and lands and whatever belongs to the same church under my protection. Wherefore I forbid that anyone unlawfully do injustice or harm to them; and they may [not] plead or answer pleas which pertain to my royal authority, unless they are in my presence, or in the presence of my supreme justice, and my officials of Beriwck are not to implead their men.
As witnesses: Walter son of Alan, steward; Richard de Morville, constable; David Oliphant.
